instruction: Implement is_balanced(s): return True iff every bracket in s is correctly matched and nested across the three bracket types (), [], {}. Examples: '([]{})' -> True, '([)]' -> False, '(' -> False, '' -> True.

files.test_brackets.py:
from brackets import is_balanced


def test_balanced():
    assert is_balanced("([]{})") is True
    assert is_balanced("([)]") is False
    assert is_balanced("(") is False
    assert is_balanced("") is True
